item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing ARD, the collapsible ruined it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The main moral I'm drawing from this is it's probably time to nuke Bigfoot again.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconded. These are two separate stories which fail to fit the same article, and the attempt drags down the weight either one could carry.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A short read, but a excellent one! +1 for you :).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article starts strong and then torpedoes itself with the collapsible. It should be about the life and times of an immortal who has to piece together his own history. Instead, you awkwardly pivot to the horrors of genocide, barely expand upon what was already said in 1000, and give little or no thought to 5888's role in that event or how it defined him. Bright's Proposal and SCP-4000 explore the horror and impact genocide in a much more dynamic and meaningful way. If you want to explore genocide, do that in a different article. Don't shackle this one to your clumsy attempt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty good, barring a few phraseologies I personally wouldn't use, but it feels a bit like SCP-1000 II: The Revenging. Knowledge of 1000 is almost totally necessary to appreciate the final log, which isn't exactly unfair given how popular 1000 itself is. But it doesn't exactly do anything really transformative, and the ending just re-states the plot and themes of 1000.

The final line is pretty cool, but what exactly will humanity be repeating? Are they genociding the anomalous, or Bigfoot again? Are the Bigfoots the ones who haven't learned from history?

It's a cool exercise in character building — I 100% want to know more about this guy or use him in an article — but I feel like I have to novote based on the ending.

item.title: Re: Just awesome
item.content:encoded: I think you mean SCP-1000, but I'm happy that you enjoyed it so much!

item.title: Just awesome
item.content:encoded: What can I say? This is extremely well crafted and was a joy to read through. It ties into SCP-2000 so well and gave me chills. Bravo! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Big thanks to cybersqyd for crit. Hope yall enjoy it.

Also, gigantic thanks to the Triple B Show and their fantastic READING of SCP-5888! Check out their channel for this and other SCP readings!

- THE GAME
  • old.jpg is in the Public Domain and can be found here. (Since it was published before Pixabay changed their licensing policy, the work is still in the Public Domain.)
  • tablet.jpg is in the Public Domain and can be found here. I slightly modified it by giving it a new background.
  • book.jpg is licensed under CC 2.0 and can be found here.
  • flowers.jpg is licensed under CC 2.0 and can be found here. I modified it by added flower iconography.

The term "Pariah King" is a reference to a song by The Shins, I do not claim ownership over this phrase.

Notice: This article has undergone a rewrite to Addendum 2; the old Addendum has been consolidated to an Item tab. To read the SCP-1000 version, please refer to the article history.
